commands.review_summary:
The PR threads configured `agents.list[].identity.name` into agent Runtime prompt metadata, renders it as `agent=...` while preserving the technical slot id as `agentId=...` when different, and adds focused prompt tests.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +55. Total +80 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level behavior: current main renders `runtimeInfo.agentId` as `agent=...` and does not resolve `identity.name`. I did not live-reproduce the intermittent multi-session model drift.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is queued: the latest patch clears the earlier code/proof blockers, so the remaining action is normal maintainer review and required-check gating.

target.title: fix(agents): render identity name in runtime prompt
target.body:
## Summary
- resolve the active agent identity.name into system prompt runtime metadata
- render the configured identity as `agent=...` while keeping the technical slot id as `agentId=...` for debugging
- add focused coverage for prompt params, runtime rendering, and CLI prompt assembly

Fixes #81269.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/system-prompt-params.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Runtime prompt metadata now prefers the configured agent identity name over the technical slot id, avoiding repeated `agent=main` persona drift when `identity.name` is set.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch (`codex/runtime-identity-name-81269-reopen-20260618`) with repository dependencies available, exercising the agents prompt parameter, prompt rendering, and CLI prompt assembly paths through the repository test harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/system-prompt-params.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts --reporter=verbose
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw agents prompt run showed the changed behavior:

```text
✓ |agents| src/agents/system-prompt-params.test.ts > buildSystemPromptParams > resolves configured identity name for the active agent
✓ |unit-fast| src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts > buildAgentSystemPrompt > includes agent and session identity in runtime when provided
✓ |agents| src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts > buildCliAgentSystemPrompt > includes session identity in runtime when provided

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  89 passed (89)
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  14 passed (14)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 6.51s
```

Observed result after fix: The focused agents tests verify configured `identity.name` is resolved for the active agent and appears in runtime prompt identity metadata while the technical slot id remains available for session/debug context. `git diff --check` produced no output.

What was not tested: A live agent conversation was not started in this environment; the focused prompt construction and CLI assembly paths above exercise the affected runtime prompt output.

---
Reopened after active-PR limit cleanup. Supersedes closed PR #94397.



## Additional proof for normalized agent ids

After commit `8a83345a17`, I verified that a configured raw id still resolves when the runtime uses the normalized id.

Command:

```text
node --import tsx -e 'import { buildSystemPromptParams } from "./src/agents/system-prompt-params.ts"; import { buildRuntimeLine } from "./src/agents/system-prompt.ts"; const { runtimeInfo } = buildSystemPromptParams({ config: { agents: { list: [{ id: "Team Ops", identity: { name: "Ops Navigator" } }] } }, agentId: "team-ops", runtime: { host: "host", os: "darwin", arch: "arm64", node: process.version, model: "test-model" } }); console.log(buildRuntimeLine(runtimeInfo));'
```

Observed output:

```text
Runtime: agent=Ops Navigator | agentId=team-ops | host=host | os=darwin (arm64) | node=v25.9.0 | model=test-model | thinking=off
```

Focused validation after the normalized-id fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/system-prompt-params.test.ts --reporter=dot
# 1 file passed, 8 tests passed

git diff --check
# no output
```
target.author: leno23

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
